name: Atlas of Australian Languages — data release

source_datasets.name: AIATSIS AUSTLANG
source_datasets.role: canonical AU language codes, approx. coordinates, alt-names

source_datasets.name: PHOIBLE
source_datasets.role: phonological inventories (pointer; not ingested in P0)

source_datasets.name: E. M. Curr, The Australian Race (1886-87)
source_datasets.role: historical locality wordlists (live DB curr_* + appendix)

source_datasets.name: English Wiktionary
source_datasets.role: open lexical resources for some languoids

caveats: A spreading language lineage is NOT a moving/arriving population. Bouckaert root age ~5,578 BP dates the Pama-Nyungan LINGUISTIC spread across an already long-populated continent (~65,000 yr of presence).
state/jurisdiction is null for all languoids: not present in the open Glottolog/AUSTLANG exports.
Autonyms are UNVERIFIED candidates drawn from alt-names (which conflate endonyms with spelling variants); never asserted as confirmed.
Derived-centroid coordinates are a drawing convenience (mean of sibling-leaf coords), not a claim about where a language is spoken; flagged approximate + provenance=derived_centroid.
Curr 1886-87 OCR wordlists are historical sources, not modern language identities.
